CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-30-2017 Continued To Sep-13-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-22-2017 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Defendant Homebridge, Inc.'s demurrer to the first, third, fourth and fifth causes of action in the first amended complaint is sustained with thirty days leave to amend as to the first, third and fourth causes of action and overruled as to the fifth cause of action. Plaintiffs acknowledge that they have not adequately alleged standing per CCP 337.32 for their survival claims (the first through fourth causes of action) and are given leave to remedy this deficiency if they can do so in good faith. The fifth cause of action adequately alleges a claim for wrongful death. Liberally construed, the fifth cause of action sufficiently alleges that Homebridge is liable for the wrongful conduct of its employee that occurred in the scope of the employment that was a substantial factor in Ms. Ward's death. For guidance of the parties going forward, the label on the third cause of action is unclear and probably should be changed to negligence and the fourth cause of action does not sufficiently allege ultimate facts to support a claim for negligent hiring, training and supervision (CACI 426), but, once the standing issue is corrected, it appears that the first and third causes of action will withstand a demurrer. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 =302/hk
